findings: The watchdog no longer recursively duplicates the full runner/checker/editorial/agentic stack during its own repair phase; it now bounds repair to runner+verifier and exits on persistent broken state.
Per-child timeouts were cut from 420s to 120s, which materially reduces cron overrun risk when agentic review/verifier paths stall.
A recursion guard was added so any accidental self-reentry reports a broken state immediately instead of chewing through nested loops.
Manual foreground run now finishes in about 36 seconds and reports broken state cleanly instead of timing out.
repairsApplied: Updated docs_stack_temp_watchdog.py to pass a watchdog child env flag, shorten child timeouts, and avoid redundant direct checker/editorial/agentic reruns inside aggressive_repair_passes().
Added tests covering bounded repair behavior and recursion-guard failure mode in test_docs_stack_temp_watchdog.py.
proof: time python3 /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/agents/docs_quality/docs_stack_temp_watchdog.py -> exits 1 in ~36s with explicit broken reasons, not timeout.
Cron-like env run still hung past 90s with no output, so true cron robustness is not yet proven.
blockers: The cron-like minimal-environment invocation did not complete within the observation window, which suggests another downstream script path may still block under cron conditions.
Because that cron-style run was not proven to finish reliably, the watchdog cannot be approved as robust under real cron conditions yet.
